tags.description: Most famous site in Cousteau Reserve featuring coral carpets at 3m, drop-off with tropical fauna, Jacques Cousteau bust at 12m depth
Sheltered pool between two islets with shallow water, rocky seabed, perfect for beginners and children
Geothermal site with warm seabed vents from Bouillante volcanic fault zone, fault formations, large spiny lobsters (langouste royale) in crevices, depth ~15m
Shore entry from Malendure black volcanic sand beach, guide rope descent through seagrass to reef at two platforms (~15m and ~20m), barracuda, snapper, lionfish, hawksbill turtles
Shallow reef 4-18m, formed from collapsed Malendure rock, popular for night dives, nocturnal species include astrophyton basket stars and corallimorphaires, also done as day dive
Shore entry from Plage Caraïbe in Pointe-Noire, calm bay, coral gardens, moray eels, crustaceans, sea turtles, operated by Anse Caraïbe Plongée (est. 2000)
Protected bay reef below Sugarloaf hill on Terre-de-Haut; sea fans, sponges, surgeon fish, butterfly fish, turtles; snorkel also possible from beach ends; rocky seabed with gorgonians and sabella

tags.seamark:name: La Broumine
tags.qa_note: No site-specific dive documentation found in 2026-05-31 QA pass. OSM marks mast showing. Location in Les Saintes waters confirmed by coordinates. Vessel identity unknown.

tags.name: Epave de l'Îlet Fortune
tags.qa_note: Vessel identity not definitively established. Located northeast of Îlet Fortune off Goyave. Portions reportedly near or at surface. No dedicated dive site documentation found.

tags.name: Épave de Caret
tags.description: Old metal sand-dredging boat deliberately sunk in Grand Cul-de-Sac Marin (marine reserve since 1987) as fish aggregation device; very shallow (2-3m to hull surface), visible from above; algae, tropical fish colonized; penetration prohibited due to structural instability and fire coral

tags.name: Pointe à Cabrit
tags.description: Multi-level dive at Îlet à Cabrit entrance to Baie des Saintes; always sheltered; shallow plateau 2-5m for beginners with scorpionfish, lobsters, trumpetfish, moray eels; drop-off to 20m; garden eels in sandy floor; La Patate Cabrit pinnacle (6-8m) nearby

tags.name: Le Sec Pâté
tags.description: Wall dive on south edge of Les Saintes archipelago (OSM node, distinct from the celebrated Sec Pâté pinnacle in the Saintes Channel). Advanced certification required, strong currents possible. Fewer than 3 independent sources specifically for this OSM node vs. the main pinnacle.
tags.qa_note: This OSM node at 15.90N may be the same feature as the main Sec Pâté pinnacle or a related wall. Cannot confirm as independently validated with 3 sources distinct from the main pinnacle site.

tags.name: La Baleine
tags.description: Wall dive in Passe de la Baleine, Les Saintes. Contains wreck of French Navy schooner Anémone (built 1823 in Bayonne, sunk hurricane Sept 7-8 1824, all 29 crew died including Captain Guillotin). Wreck at ~25m, archaeologically excavated 2015-2019 (554 artifacts). Vessel used to patrol against illicit slave trade.

tags.name: Pointe du Gouvernail
tags.description: Wall dive off Terre-de-Bas, Les Saintes; drop-off starts at 4m descends to 20-22m; abundant predators: barracuda, frigate tuna, bigeye jacks (carangues); queen triggerfish, groupers, large green moray eels, lobsters in rifts, black fan corals, sponges

tags.name: L'Aquarium
tags.description: Wall/tunnel dive in Les Saintes; tunnels and plateaus covered with corals and sponges; schools of gorettes (French grunts), cardinal fish, angelfish, wrasses; impressive underwater architecture

tags.name: Le Gustavia
tags.description: Cargo vessel: built 1950 in Germany (PAHL SCHIFFSWERFT) as 'Hamburg'; renamed Drochtersen (1963), Geocéanique (1975), Gustavia (1982, owned La Cascade shipping). 48.32m × 7.6m, 416 tonnes. Damaged Hurricane Hugo Sept 17 1989 in Pointe-à-Pitre port; sunk as artificial reef May 1991 ~1 mile from Malendure, upright on sandy bottom at 40m. Rich in sponges, barracuda, groupers, moray eels. Requires PE40/Advanced certification.

tags.name: Le Franjack
tags.description: Suction dredger: built 1958 at Danyard-Frederikshavns, Denmark as 'Oresund'; renamed Franjack 1976 (for owner Lacascade's sons François and Jacky); 53.32m × 10.04m, 486 tonnes. Damaged Hurricane Hugo 1989; sunk as artificial reef June 9 1996 at ~23m on sandy bottom near Malendure. Engine room, wheelhouse penetrable. Eels, shrimp, sponges, bryozoans, anemones resident; forward cabin collapsed. Sources give length as 50-53m; PADI lists 45m (possibly underestimate).

tags.name: L'Augustin Fresnel II
tags.description: Beacon tender (baliseur): built ~1940 in Canada; 53m × 9m, 660 tonnes. Served Mediterranean (Marseille, Corsica) then transferred to Pointe-à-Pitre 1990 to maintain Antilles-Guyane marine markers; retired 1994. Sunk as artificial reef July 9 2003 at ~29-33m. 15 cabins and gangways accessible. Stingrays, barracudas, moray eels, turtles, groupers, scorpionfish, nudibranchs, trevallies. Requires Advanced/CMAS 2.
Les îlets à Frégate (Petit Cul-de-Sac Marin) ont disparu (semble-t-il après le passage du cyclone de 1928) et sont désormais des récifs connus sous le nom Frégate de Haut et Frégate de Bas.
tags.name: Frégate-de-Haut
tags.qa_note: Reef confirmed to exist (former îlet submerged after 1928 hurricane) but no dive-specific documentation with 3 independent sources found in QA pass. OSM marks as natural reef. Location in Petit Cul-de-Sac Marin confirmed.

tags.description: Les îlets à Frégate (Petit Cul-de-Sac Marin) ont disparu (semble-t-il après le passage du cyclone de 1928) et sont désormais des récifs connus sous le nom Frégate de Haut et Frégate de Bas.
tags.name: Frégate-de-Bas
tags.qa_note: Same as Frégate-de-Haut — reef confirmed but no dive-specific documentation with 3 independent sources found.

tags.name: Caye Fortune
tags.qa_note: OSM natural=reef only. No dive-specific documentation found in QA pass. Depth placeholder.

tags.name: Caye Martinique
tags.qa_note: OSM natural=reef only. No dive-specific documentation found in QA pass. Depth placeholder.

tags.name: Caye Tome
tags.qa_note: OSM natural=reef only. No dive-specific documentation found in QA pass. Depth placeholder.

tags.seamark:name: Lynndy
tags.description: Small coastal freighter submerged 2006 after tropical storm in Anse Mire, Terre-de-Haut, Les Saintes; rests at 15m (~50ft) ~100m from beach; marked with two yellow cross buoys; minimal coral encrustation as of documentation; bluehead wrasse, moray eels, trunkfish; OSM marks 'dangerous' (likely shallow hazard). Only accessible to divers/freedivers — too deep to snorkel from surface. Difficulty adjusted to Intermediate (15m, not yet well-encrusted).

tags.name: Caye à l'Anglais
tags.qa_note: OSM natural=reef only. No dive-specific documentation found in QA pass. Depth placeholder.

tags.name: Caye Gros Loup
tags.qa_note: OSM natural=reef only. No dive-specific documentation found in QA pass. Depth placeholder.

tags.description: Legendary submerged volcanic pinnacle in Canal des Saintes; summit 15m, drops to 300m; three peaks; intense 1-4 knot currents; colorful sponges and soft corals; sea turtles, angelfish, surgeonfish, barracuda, trevally, humpback whales (seasonal). Requires CMAS 2/AOW + Deep Diver + 50 logged dives + PE40.

tags.qa_note: Location confirmed as geothermally active cape north of Bouillante bay, classified as protected coastal zone 2009. Sandy bottom with mooring confirmed. Dived by local enthusiasts per sea-seek.com. However, no dedicated dive site guide with depth/marine life data found. Cannot confirm 3 independent dive-specific sources.
